data.description:
Two gate defects this cycle (no_repo_root code-root drop; the prompted-structured verifier emitting an unschema'd shape that tolerant-parse silently dropped to empty → all findings `no-verification`) shared a root pattern: **a load-bearing contract between stages was implicit and only weakly enforced**, so a break degraded silently instead of failing loudly, and our tests could not catch it without brittle grep/glob/string heuristics. This story closes that CLASS for plan-review and the other LLM workflows.

## Why
- The verify→decide contract ("each finding gets a verification keyed by integer `index`") was implicit; a verifier returning a divergent shape produced an EMPTY-but-valid object (extra keys ignored), so the failure was invisible until findings mysteriously all went `no-verification`.
- The code-read-root contract (which snapshot a stage reads) was threaded ad-hoc across five hops and silently became `None`.
- Current gate self-tests lean on greps/globs/string-contains (e.g. allowlist↔doc lock-step, stale-path sweeps), which are brittle and miss structural breaks.
We want STRONG, EXPLICIT, TESTABLE, ENFORCEABLE contracts at the stage seams — so a break is a loud, deterministic test failure, not a silent degrade.

## What (research-informed; authorized to research OSS)
- Research popular, actively-maintained OSS that run multi-stage LLM/agent pipelines and structured-output contracts (candidates to evaluate, not prescriptive: Pydantic AI, Instructor, Outlines/Guidance/`jsonschema`-strict modes, LangGraph/LangChain runnable schemas, DSPy signatures, Marvin, BAML, Guardrails) for how they (a) make inter-stage contracts explicit, (b) fail loudly on contract drift, and (c) test the seams without string heuristics. Capture findings + cited patterns in a comment/ADR.
- Define explicit contracts at the LLM-workflow stage seams (at minimum: the Pass-2 verification↔Pass-3 decide keying, and the code/ticket read-root each stage consumes), enforced where the data crosses the seam:
  * REJECT-don't-ignore unknown/missing keys at the structured boundary (e.g. an extra-forbid / required-`index` policy so a divergent verifier shape FAILS LOUDLY instead of yielding empty), with a clear degraded-reason distinct from a genuine "couldn't verify".
  * a NEGATIVE/property test per seam that a contract break is detected deterministically (no grep/glob/substring assertions).
- Replace the brittle grep/glob self-checks where a structural assertion is feasible (e.g. assert the allowlist set == the computed over-cap set from a function, not a doc-string lockstep).

## Scope
In: the contract definitions + enforcement at the LLM-workflow seams, the seam tests, the OSS research writeup, and migrating the highest-value brittle grep/glob checks. Out: rewriting the workflow engine; non-LLM subsystems beyond the read-root contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] An OSS-research writeup (comment or `docs/adr/`) cites how 3+ actively-maintained projects make LLM-pipeline/structured-output contracts explicit + testable, with the patterns we adopt and why.
- [ ] The Pass-2→Pass-3 verification contract is explicit and ENFORCED at the boundary: a verifier output that does not conform (missing/duplicate `index`, wrong wrapper keys) is detected and surfaced as a distinct, loud failure — NOT silently coerced to empty/`no-verification`. Covered by a deterministic negative test.
- [ ] The code/ticket read-root contract for gate stages is asserted by a test that fails if a stage receives an unresolved/`None` root when one is required (no string heuristics).
- [ ] At least one brittle grep/glob self-check is replaced by a structural assertion (e.g. allowlist↔over-cap-set computed equality).
- [ ] No new test relies on grep/glob/substring-contains for a structural contract; `make check` + the suite pass.
data.title: Enforceable, testable contracts at LLM-workflow stage seams (kill the silent-degrade class)
